text.title: Lessons Storage, Searchability, and Application
text.summary: Requirements for a searchable, versioned lessons repository; application SLO definition; staleness expiry rules.

rule.statement: Store lessons in a versioned repository (git or equivalent) with a controlled tag taxonomy. Inconsistent tags make lessons unfindable at kickoff retrieval.
rule.rationale: A searchable corpus requires consistent categorization; without it, semantic search retrieves shallow matches and the repository becomes a graveyard.

rule.statement: Add a last_validated_on field to every lesson. Review tooling-specific lessons annually; expire at 24 months. Process and team lessons expire at 5 years. Mark expired lessons as archived, not deleted.
rule.rationale: Tooling lessons go stale quickly as stacks change; recommending a deprecated approach is worse than no recommendation.

rule.statement: At every project kickoff, retrieve relevant lessons by tag and similarity and insert them into the planning checklist. Track application rate: of last quarter's lessons, what percent triggered a documented process change? Target: above 50%.
rule.rationale: Lessons that are captured but not applied provide zero value; the application rate is the only metric that distinguishes a knowledge system from a documentation exercise.

rule.statement: If application rate falls below 30% for two consecutive quarters, declare the storage or retrieval system broken and redesign the workflow before adding more lessons.
rule.rationale: Adding more lessons to an unused system increases storage cost without benefit; the constraint is retrieval and application, not volume.

antipattern.description: Lessons held only at project close-out.
antipattern.reason: Small, frequent lessons are forgotten over a multi-month project; by close-out only major events are recalled, and even those lose context details.

antipattern.description: Lessons stored in a wiki without a tag taxonomy or search capability.
antipattern.reason: An unsearchable repository means lessons cannot be retrieved at kickoff; they effectively do not exist for the next project team.

antipattern.description: Recommendations written as "consider Y" or "be more careful".
antipattern.reason: Vague recommendations cannot be actioned, measured, or assigned. Every recommendation requires an action verb, an owner role, and a point of application.

see-also.link: Vendor-specific lessons inform the approved-vendor list and next RFP criteria
